chapter_title.ar: بَابُ حَدِّ الْمَرَضِ الَّذِي يَجُوزُ لِلرَّجُلِ أَنْ يُفْطِرَ فِيهِ
chapter_title.en: Chapter 39 – The limit of the illness which is allowed for the man that he does not Fast therein

gradings: Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/306)
Allamah Baqir al-Majlisi: حسن - Mir‘at al ‘Uqul Fi Sharh Akhbar Al al Rasul (16/306)
text: حُمِمْتُ بِالْمَدِينَةِ يَوْماً فِي شَهْرِ رَمَضَانَ فَبَعَثَ إِلَيَّ أَبُو عَبْدِ اللَّهِ ( عليه السلام ) بِقَصْعَةٍ فِيهَا خَلٌّ وَ زَيْتٌ وَ قَالَ أَفْطِرْ وَ صَلِّ وَ أَنْتَ قَاعِدٌ .

en.hubeali: Ali Bin Ibrahim, from his father, from Ibn Abu Umeyr, from Jameel Bin Darraj, from Al Waleed Bin Sabeeh who said,
‘I had fever in Al-Medina one day during the Month of Ramazan. So, Abu Abdullah<sup>asws</sup> sent to me a basket wherein was vinegar and oil, and said: ‘Break Fast, and Pray while you are seated’.
en.sarwar: 1. Ali ibn Ibrahim has narrated from his father from ibn abu ‘Umayr from Jamil ibn Darraj from al-Walid ibn Sabih who has said the following: “Once in al-Madinah, during the month of Ramadan, I had fever, and abu ‘ Abd Allah, ‘Alayhi al- Salam, sent a bowl with olive oil and vinegar in it and said, ’Eat and perform Salat (prayer) in a sitting position.”’
